reliability: High (CBS News, Al Jazeera, HuffPost, Economic Times)
scanType: Overnight update: Geopolitics (Iran war, prediction market impact)
summary: Iran REJECTED the US 15-point ceasefire proposal. Called talks 'illogical.' No negotiations planned. Witkoff/Kushner diplomatic push has stalled.

Trump extended pause on Iranian energy infrastructure strikes (still targeting April 6 deadline). Netanyahu continuing strikes independently. Houthis hit Israel again. Fire at Kuwait airport after drones hit fuel tank. Three journalists killed in Israeli strike in Lebanon.
Oil spiking: Brent crude $101.89 (+4.8%), US crude $94.48 (+4.6%). Strait of Hormuz blockade threat ongoing. LPG shortage emerging.

Prediction market activity: Polymarket hosting 1,382 active markets. Geopolitics driving volume: Yemen markets ($1M 24h), Houthi markets ($1M 24h), UFC ($10M 24h). Iran ceasefire odds likely dropping after rejection.

Net assessment: war is escalating, not de-escalating. April 6 energy strike deadline remains but Iran's rejection makes a deal less likely. Oil above $100 creates broader economic uncertainty that drives MORE prediction market demand.

entities.name: Steve Witkoff
entities.role: US special envoy, presented 15-point peace proposal to Iran

entities.name: Jared Kushner
entities.role: Leading push for 1-month ceasefire in Iran war

marketImpact.prediction_markets: Iran rejection = more uncertainty = more trading volume. Expect geopolitics markets to spike.
marketImpact.oil: Above $100/barrel. If Strait of Hormuz blocked or energy strikes proceed April 6, could go significantly higher.
marketImpact.crypto: Bitcoin typically inverse-correlated with geopolitical risk in short term but prediction market volume is a separate driver.
marketImpact.lever_relevance: Elevated geopolitical uncertainty is the strongest possible environment for prediction market demand. LEVER launching into this environment is well-timed.

workstreamTags.CEO: Oil above $100, Iran ceasefire rejected, war escalating. Prediction market demand at peak. This is the macro backdrop for LEVER's fundraising: 'We are building leverage infrastructure for a $21B+ market that is growing because the world is uncertain, and uncertainty is not going away.'
workstreamTags.ADVISOR: April 6 remains the key date. If energy strikes proceed after Iran's rejection, expect oil price shock and a new wave of prediction market activity. LEVER should be ready to launch into this momentum, not after it fades.

sources.title: CBS News: Trump Extends Pause, Witkoff 15-Point Plan
HuffPost: Iran Rejects US Ceasefire Plan
